title: Treatment of midbrain organoids with αSyn-PFFs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e9egs","text":"This protocol describes how to temporarily transfer midbrain (or other brain-region) organoids into multi-well plates to perform short-term exposure to α-synuclein pre-formed fibrils (αSyn-PFFs) or any comparable protein/aggregate treatment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"gia04","text":"Midbrain organoids were generated according to the here described tri-phasic Wnt modulation protocol and bioreactor cultivation method. At DIV 80, organoids were selected for αSyn-PFF treatments. Individual Wnt-TriP + bioreactor organoids were carefully transferred from the bioreactor using a cut pipette tip to a 48-well cell culture plate (Corning, NY, USA). To ensure homogenous treatment conditions, one organoid was placed in one well each. For treatments, organoids received either αSyn-PFFs at a final concentration of 5μg/ml or an equivalent volume of TRIS-Saline-Buffer as controls. To ensure uniform exposure to αSyn-PFFs, organoids were gently flipped daily and maintained under static conditions for 48 hours at 37°C in a humidified incubator with 5% CO2. After 48 hours, organoids were transferred back to the bioreactor and supplemented with fresh medium. To account for any potential stress or damage during handling, TRIS-Saline-Buffer treated control organoids underwent identical transfer to 48-well-plates, medium changes, and manipulation procedures throughout the experiment. Organoid culture medium was refreshed every 3 days.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Gently harvest individual organoids from their culture vessel using a cut or wide-bore pipette tip . Transfer one organoid per well into a 48-well low-attachment plate containing pre-warmed medium. Allow organoids to equilibrate for 15–30 minutes at 37 °C.

This protocol describes how to temporarily transfer midbrain (or other brain-region) organoids into multi-well plates to perform short-term exposure to α-synuclein pre-formed fibrils (αSyn-PFFs)or any comparable protein/aggregate treatment. . Ensure sterility throughout the procedure. Use wide-bore or cut pipette tips to avoid damaging organoids during transfer
Prepare the αSyn-PFF working solution at the desired final concentration (e.g., 5 µg/mL). Prepare an identical volume of vehicle/control buffer for control wells.
Carefully remove most of the medium from each well without disturbing the organoid.
Add αSyn-PFF solution to treatment wells and vehicle buffer to control wells.
Maintain organoids under static conditions at 37 °C, 5% CO₂ for the entire exposure period (e.g., 48 hours).
To ensure uniform exposure, gently rotate or flip organoids once per day.

After the exposure period, gently transfer organoids back to their original culture system: - Bioreactor or spinning culture if previously grown dynamically - Add fresh, pre-warmed organoid medium. - Process control organoids identically , including all transfers and medium changes.
creator: Hariam Raji
